technical.body: S&P 500 7,230.12 (+0.29%) new record, Nasdaq 25,114.44 (+0.89%) new record (first ever close above 25,000); both posted weekly gains and capped April best month since 2020; AAPL +3.28% to $280.25 on the day after Q2 print; Mag-7 super-week closed cleanly with Apple +5% intraday on iPhone $57B (+22%) + Services $30.97B (+16%) + Greater China $20.5B (+28%) + $100B buyback authorization + Q3 guide +14-17% (vs 9.5% consensus); Dow -0.31% to 49,499.27 the lone laggard — — Apple's after-print rally pulled the Nasdaq through the 25K barrier on the same session AAPL closed at $280.25. The +14-17% Q3 guide was the structural number; it priced the iPhone-17 super-cycle into the May tape and gave the buyside a clean print to absorb the Apr 28 WSJ OpenAI-revenue-miss noise.

citations.label: The Motley Fool - Stock Market Today, May 1: Apple Jumps After Record Quarter and $100 Billion Share Buyback

technical.body: control plane for AI agents at $15/user/month, included in Microsoft 365 E7 SKU; three pillars (observe / govern / secure); tools management gives admins centralized view + allow/block for Microsoft MCP servers across the tenant; integrates with Entra ID + Purview + Intune + Defender; June 2026 Defender adds asset-context mapping per agent (devices, MCP servers, identities, cloud resources); GCC + GCC High availability slated late 2026, DoD early 2027 — — First mainstream-priced enterprise control plane for the agent fleet at the M365 admin layer; structurally pairs with Apr 30 Microsoft Foundry Agent Framework 1.0 GA + Foundry Toolbox preview as the vendor-side enforcement substrate. The MCP-server allow/block primitive is the missing enterprise gate that turns the Apr 28 ATT&CK v19 named-techniques (MCP server compromise) from a threat-model into an operational control.

citations.label: Microsoft Security Blog - Microsoft Agent 365, now generally available, expands capabilities and integrations

technical.lead: CISA KEV May 1 adds CVE-2026-31431 "Copy Fail" Linux kernel local privilege escalation (CVSS 7.8 but BOD-22-01 mandatory); federal remediation deadline May 15; algif_aead AF_ALG socket + splice() interaction enables a controlled 4-byte write into kernel page cache of any readable file, corrupting in-memory representation of /usr/bin/su to yield root without modifying the on-disk binary; affects virtually every Linux distro since 2017
technical.body: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, Amazon Linux 2023, RHEL 10.1, SUSE 16, Debian, Fedora, Arch; multiple in-the-wild attacks observed against US think tanks, European manufacturing, cloud service providers; typical chain pairs Copy Fail with a separate web-app/VPN-appliance RCE for initial foothold then Copy Fail for root — — Largest single-vendor KEV-affected install base of the cycle (every Linux machine in production from kernel 4.x onward); 14-day federal deadline is the second-tightest of 2026; the Microsoft Security Blog Apr-30/May-1 disclosure is the cross-vendor reference.

citations.label: CISA - Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog (May 1)

technical.lead: Pentagon May 1 inks classified-network AI deals with 7 companies (SpaceX, OpenAI, Google, NVIDIA, Reflection, Microsoft, AWS)
technical.body: Anthropic explicitly excluded after refusing to grant Claude access for "all lawful purposes" including fully autonomous weapons + mass domestic surveillance; DoD designated Anthropic a "supply chain risk" (label historically reserved for foreign-adversary-linked vendors); federal judge in California blocked the Trump-admin freeze last month after Anthropic sued; Trump chief of staff Susie Wiles met Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei Apr 17 at the White House, Trump told CNBC a deal was "possible" — — First publicly-disclosed presidential-administration vs frontier-AI-lab autonomous-weapons-use stand-off; sets a procurement precedent that will pull every other lab's DoD-bid posture into focus through Q3 earnings.

citations.label: Defense News - Pentagon freezes out Anthropic as it signs deals with AI rivals

technical.lead: State Department May 1 sanctions Iran-China oil trade
technical.body: designates Qingdao Haiye Oil Terminal Co. (China-based petroleum terminal that imported tens of millions of barrels of sanctioned Iranian crude); Treasury concurrently sanctions three Iranian currency exchange houses processing billions annually for IRGC + MODAFL; pairs with Trump's standing 25% secondary-tariff threat on any country doing business with Iran + 50% tariff threat on weapons suppliers; Foreign Policy May 1 op-ed argues USTR Section 301 Iran plan is "neither coherent nor defensible" — — Largest single-day Iran enforcement action of Q2 2026 and the first Treasury-State joint package targeting Chinese terminal operators directly; structurally tightens the Iran-China oil arbitrage that has run since the Jan 12 Trump 25% tariff announcement.

citations.label: State Department - U.S. Sanctions Tighten Grip on Iran-China Oil Trade

plain: S&P 7,230 + Nasdaq 25K records as Apple +3.28% to $280
Microsoft Agent 365 GA at $15/user controls AI agents
CISA flags Linux Copy Fail bug, May 15 federal deadline
Pentagon signs 7 AI vendors, drops Anthropic as risk
US sanctions hit China oil terminal over Iran trade
